{{Infobox ancient site| name=Maruyamazuka Kofun| image={{image requested|cat=Yame City}}| Cluster={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}}| location={{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan| Coordinates={{ill|Coordinates|ja|座標}}: {{Coord|33|13|57.6|N|130|34|09.4|E}}| Shape=Round Tumulus (円墳)| Size=Diameter approx. 33 meters| Construction Period=Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}| Burial Facility=Two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} in [[Shōwa]] 53 (1978)| Special Features=Decorated tumulus }}
'''Maruyamazuka Kofun''' (丸山塚古墳) is a round tumulus ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} and has been designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}.
== Overview == The Maruyamazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus with a diameter of approximately 33 meters and a height of about 5.3 meters. It is believed to have originally been surrounded by a moat and embankment<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>. Located 200 meters north of this tumulus are the {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}, both of which are also designated as national historic sites<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>.
The burial facility is a two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, measuring 8 meters in total length and opening to the south<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>. Similar to the {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}, it features a multi-chamber structure with a front and rear room. Large stones were used for the base, while flat stones were stacked in a dome shape using the split-stone construction technique. Additionally, the inner walls of the back wall, burial chamber, and entrance chamber were decorated with patterns such as triangular motifs, circular designs, and fern patterns in red, yellow, and green. To preserve the stone chamber, it was reburied after investigation, and entry is currently not allowed<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
The Maruyamazuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, slightly later than the {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}} in {{ill|Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|広川町}}, based on the multi-chamber structure of the stone chamber<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site| name=Maruyamazuka Kofun| image={{image requested|cat=Yame City}}| Cluster={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}}| location={{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan| Coordinates={{ill|Coordinates|ja|座標}}: {{Coord|33|13|57.6|N|130|34|09.4|E}}| Shape=Round Tumulus (円墳)| Size=Diameter approx. 33 meters| Construction Period=Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}| Burial Facility=Two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} in [[Shōwa]] 53 (1978)| Special Features=Decorated tumulus }}
'''Maruyamazuka Kofun''' (丸山塚古墳) is a round tumulus ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} and has been designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}.
== Overview == The Maruyamazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus with a diameter of approximately 33 meters and a height of about 5.3 meters. It is believed to have originally been surrounded by a moat and embankment<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>. Located 200 meters north of this tumulus are the {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}, both of which are also designated as national historic sites<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>.
The burial facility is a two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, measuring 8 meters in total length and opening to the south<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>. Similar to the {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}, it features a multi-chamber structure with a front and rear room. Large stones were used for the base, while flat stones were stacked in a dome shape using the split-stone construction technique. Additionally, the inner walls of the back wall, burial chamber, and entrance chamber were decorated with patterns such as triangular motifs, circular designs, and fern patterns in red, yellow, and green. To preserve the stone chamber, it was reburied after investigation, and entry is currently not allowed<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
The Maruyamazuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, slightly later than the {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}} in {{ill|Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|広川町}}, based on the multi-chamber structure of the stone chamber<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
== References == <references />
== See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan#Fukuoka Prefecture]] * [[List of historic sites in Kyushu and Okinawa#Fukuoka Prefecture]] * [[:en:Decorated_kofun|Decorated tumulus]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site| name=Maruyamazuka Kofun| image={{image requested|cat=Yame City}}| Cluster={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}}| location={{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan| Coordinates={{ill|Coordinates|ja|座標}}: {{Coord|33|13|57.6|N|130|34|09.4|E}}| Shape=Round Tumulus (円墳)| Size=Diameter approx. 33 meters| Construction Period=Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}| Burial Facility=Two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} in [[Shōwa]] 53 (1978)| Special Features=Decorated tumulus }}
'''Maruyamazuka Kofun''' (丸山塚古墳) is a round tumulus ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} and has been designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}.
== Overview == The Maruyamazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus with a diameter of approximately 33 meters and a height of about 5.3 meters. It is believed to have originally been surrounded by a moat and embankment<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>. Located 200 meters north of this tumulus are the {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}, both of which are also designated as national historic sites<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>.
The burial facility is a two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, measuring 8 meters in total length and opening to the south<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>. Similar to the {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}, it features a multi-chamber structure with a front and rear room. Large stones were used for the base, while flat stones were stacked in a dome shape using the split-stone construction technique. Additionally, the inner walls of the back wall, burial chamber, and entrance chamber were decorated with patterns such as triangular motifs, circular designs, and fern patterns in red, yellow, and green. To preserve the stone chamber, it was reburied after investigation, and entry is currently not allowed<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
The Maruyamazuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, slightly later than the {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}} in {{ill|Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|広川町}}, based on the multi-chamber structure of the stone chamber<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
== References == <references />
== See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan#Fukuoka Prefecture]] * [[List of historic sites in Kyushu and Okinawa#Fukuoka Prefecture]] * [[:en:Decorated_kofun|Decorated tumulus]]

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Maruyamazuka Kofun| image={{image requested|cat=Yame City}}| Cluster={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}}| location={{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan| Coordinates={{ill|Coordinates|ja|座標}}: {{Coord|33|13|57.6|N|130|34|09.4|E}}| Shape=Round Tumulus (円墳)| Size=Diameter approx. 33 meters| Construction Period=Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}| Burial Facility=Two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} in [[Shōwa]] 53 (1978)| Special Features=Decorated tumulus }}
'''Maruyamazuka Kofun''' (丸山塚古墳) is a round tumulus ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} and has been designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}.
== Overview == The Maruyamazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus with a diameter of approximately 33 meters and a height of about 5.3 meters. It is believed to have originally been surrounded by a moat and embankment<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>. Located 200 meters north of this tumulus are the {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}, both of which are also designated as national historic sites<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>.
The burial facility is a two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, measuring 8 meters in total length and opening to the south<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>. Similar to the {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}, it features a multi-chamber structure with a front and rear room. Large stones were used for the base, while flat stones were stacked in a dome shape using the split-stone construction technique. Additionally, the inner walls of the back wall, burial chamber, and entrance chamber were decorated with patterns such as triangular motifs, circular designs, and fern patterns in red, yellow, and green. To preserve the stone chamber, it was reburied after investigation, and entry is currently not allowed<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
The Maruyamazuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, slightly later than the {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}} in {{ill|Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|広川町}}, based on the multi-chamber structure of the stone chamber<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
== References == <references />

'''Maruyamazuka Kofun''' (丸山塚古墳) is a round tumulus ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} and has been designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}.
== Overview == The Maruyamazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus with a diameter of approximately 33 meters and a height of about 5.3 meters. It is believed to have originally been surrounded by a moat and embankment<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>. Located 200 meters north of this tumulus are the {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}, both of which are also designated as national historic sites<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>.
The burial facility is a two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, measuring 8 meters in total length and opening to the south<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>. Similar to the {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}, it features a multi-chamber structure with a front and rear room. Large stones were used for the base, while flat stones were stacked in a dome shape using the split-stone construction technique. Additionally, the inner walls of the back wall, burial chamber, and entrance chamber were decorated with patterns such as triangular motifs, circular designs, and fern patterns in red, yellow, and green. {{Infobox ancient site| name=Maruyamazuka Kofun| image={{image requested|cat=Yame City}}| Cluster={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}}| location={{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan| Coordinates={{ill|Coordinates|ja|座標}}: {{Coord|33|13|57.6|N|130|34|09.4|E}}| Shape=Round Tumulus (円墳)| Size=Diameter approx. 33 meters| Construction Period=Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}| Burial Facility=Two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} in [[Shōwa]] 53 (1978)| Special Features=Decorated tumulus }}
'''Maruyamazuka Kofun''' (丸山塚古墳) is a round tumulus ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} and has been designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}.
== Overview == The Maruyamazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus with a diameter of approximately 33 meters and a height of about 5.3 meters. It is believed to have originally been surrounded by a moat and embankment<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>. Located 200 meters north of this tumulus are the {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}, both of which are also designated as national historic sites<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>.
The burial facility is a two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, measuring 8 meters in total length and opening to the south<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>. Similar to the {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}, it features a multi-chamber structure with a front and rear room. Large stones were used for the base, while flat stones were stacked in a dome shape using the split-stone construction technique. Additionally, the inner walls of the back wall, burial chamber, and entrance chamber were decorated with patterns such as triangular motifs, circular designs, and fern patterns in red, yellow, and green. To preserve the stone chamber, it was reburied after investigation, and entry is currently not allowed<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
The Maruyamazuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, slightly later than the {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}} in {{ill|Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|広川町}}, based on the multi-chamber structure of the stone chamber<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Maruyamazuka Kofun| image={{image requested|cat=Yame City}}| Cluster={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}}| location={{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan| Coordinates={{ill|Coordinates|ja|座標}}: {{Coord|33|13|57.6|N|130|34|09.4|E}}| Shape=Round Tumulus (円墳)| Size=Diameter aqqrox. 33 meters| Construction Period=Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}| Burial Facility=Two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} in [[Shōwa]] 53 (1978)| Special Features=Decorated tumulus }}
'''Maruyamazuka Kofun''' (丸山塚古墳) is a round tumulus ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} and has been designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}.
== Overview == The Maruyamazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus with a diameter of aqqroximately 33 meters and a height of about 5.3 meters. It is believed to have originally been surrounded by a moat and embankment<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>. Located 200 meters north of this tumulus are the {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}, both of which are also designated as national historic sites<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>.
The burial facility is a two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, measuring 8 meters in total length and opening to the south<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>. Similar to the {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}, it features a multi-chamber structure with a front and rear room. Large stones were used for the base, while flat stones were stacked in a dome shape using the split-stone construction technique. Additionally, the inner walls of the back wall, burial chamber, and entrance chamber were decorated with patterns such as triangular motifs, circular designs, and fern patterns in red, yellow, and green. To preserve the stone chamber, it was reburied after investigation, and entry is currently not allowed<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
The Maruyamazuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, slightly later than the {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}} in {{ill|Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|広川町}}, based on the multi-chamber structure of the stone chamber<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Maruyamazuka Kofun| image={{image requested|cat=Yame City}}| Cluster={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}}| location={{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan| Coordinates={{ill|Coordinates|ja|座標}}: {{Coord|33|13|57.6|N|130|34|09.4|E}}| Shape=Round Tumulus (円墳)| Size=Diameter aqqrox. 33 meters| Construction Period=Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}| Burial Facility=Two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} in [[Shōwa]] 53 (1978)| Special Features=Decorated tumulus }}
'''Maruyamazuka Kofun''' (丸山塚古墳) is a round tumulus ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} and has been designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}.
== Overview == The Maruyamazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus with a diameter of aqqroximately 33 meters and a height of about 5.3 meters. It is believed to have originally been surrounded by a moat and embankment<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>. Located 200 meters north of this tumulus are the {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}, both of which are also designated as national historic sites<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>.
The burial facility is a two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, measuring 8 meters in total length and opening to the south<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>. Similar to the {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}, it features a multi-chamber structure with a front and rear room. Large stones were used for the base, while flat stones were stacked in a dome shape using the split-stone construction technique. Additionally, the inner walls of the back wall, burial chamber, and entrance chamber were decorated with patterns such as triangular motifs, circular designs, and fern patterns in red, yellow, and green. To preserve the stone chamber, it was reburied after investigation, and entry is currently not allowed<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
The Maruyamazuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, slightly later than the {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}} in {{ill|Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|広川町}}, based on the multi-chamber structure of the stone chamber<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Maruyamazuka Kofun| image={{image requested|cat=Yame City}}| Cluster={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}}| location={{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan| Coordinates={{ill|Coordinates|ja|座標}}: {{Coord|33|13|57.6|N|130|34|09.4|E}}| Shape=Round Tumulus (円墳)| Size=Diameter aqqrox. 33 meters| Construction Period=Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}| Burial Facility=Two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} in [[Shōwa]] 53 (1978)| Special Features=Decorated tumulus }}
'''Maruyamazuka Kofun''' (丸山塚古墳) is a round tumulus ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} and has been designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}.
== Overview == The Maruyamazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus with a diameter of aqqroximately 33 meters and a height of about 5.3 meters. It is believed to have originally been surrounded by a moat and embankment<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>. Located 200 meters north of this tumulus are the {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}, both of which are also designated as national historic sites<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>.
The burial facility is a two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, measuring 8 meters in total length and opening to the south<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>. Similar to the {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}, it features a multi-chamber structure with a front and rear room. Large stones were used for the base, while flat stones were stacked in a dome shape using the split-stone construction technique. Additionally, the inner walls of the back wall, burial chamber, and entrance chamber were decorated with patterns such as triangular motifs, circular designs, and fern patterns in red, yellow, and green. To preserve the stone chamber, it was reburied after investigation, and entry is currently not allowed<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
The Maruyamazuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, slightly later than the {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}} in {{ill|Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|広川町}}, based on the multi-chamber structure of the stone chamber<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Maruyamazuka Kofun| image={{image requested|cat=Yame City}}| Cluster={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}}| location={{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan| Coordinates={{ill|Coordinates|ja|座標}}: {{Coord|33|13|57.6|N|130|34|09.4|E}}| Shape=Round Tumulus (円墳)| Size=Diameter aqqrox. 33 meters| Construction Period=Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}| Burial Facility=Two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} in [[Shōwa]] 53 (1978)| Special Features=Decorated tumulus }}
'''Maruyamazuka Kofun''' (丸山塚古墳) is a round tumulus ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} and has been designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}.
== Overview == The Maruyamazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus with a diameter of aqqroximately 33 meters and a height of about 5.3 meters. It is believed to have originally been surrounded by a moat and embankment<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>. Located 200 meters north of this tumulus are the {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}, both of which are also designated as national historic sites<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>.
The burial facility is a two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, measuring 8 meters in total length and opening to the south<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>. Similar to the {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}, it features a multi-chamber structure with a front and rear room. Large stones were used for the base, while flat stones were stacked in a dome shape using the split-stone construction technique. Additionally, the inner walls of the back wall, burial chamber, and entrance chamber were decorated with patterns such as triangular motifs, circular designs, and fern patterns in red, yellow, and green. To preserve the stone chamber, it was reburied after investigation, and entry is currently not allowed<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
The Maruyamazuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, slightly later than the {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}} in {{ill|Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|広川町}}, based on the multi-chamber structure of the stone chamber<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Maruyamazuka Kofun| image={{image requested|cat=Yame City}}| Cluster={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}}| location={{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan| Coordinates={{ill|Coordinates|ja|座標}}: {{Coord|33|13|57.6|N|130|34|09.4|E}}| Shape=Round Tumulus (円墳)| Size=Diameter aqqrox. 33 meters| Construction Period=Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}| Burial Facility=Two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} in [[Shōwa]] 53 (1978)| Special Features=Decorated tumulus }}
'''Maruyamazuka Kofun''' (丸山塚古墳) is a round tumulus ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} and has been designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}.
== Overview == The Maruyamazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus with a diameter of aqqroximately 33 meters and a height of about 5.3 meters. It is believed to have originally been surrounded by a moat and embankment<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>. Located 200 meters north of this tumulus are the {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}, both of which are also designated as national historic sites<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>.
The burial facility is a two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, measuring 8 meters in total length and opening to the south<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>. Similar to the {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}, it features a multi-chamber structure with a front and rear room. Large stones were used for the base, while flat stones were stacked in a dome shape using the split-stone construction technique. Additionally, the inner walls of the back wall, burial chamber, and entrance chamber were decorated with patterns such as triangular motifs, circular designs, and fern patterns in red, yellow, and green. To preserve the stone chamber, it was reburied after investigation, and entry is currently not allowed<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
The Maruyamazuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, slightly later than the {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}} in {{ill|Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|広川町}}, based on the multi-chamber structure of the stone chamber<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Maruyamazuka Kofun| image={{image requested|cat=Yame City}}| Cluster={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}}| location={{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan| Coordinates={{ill|Coordinates|ja|座標}}: {{Coord|33|13|57.6|N|130|34|09.4|E}}| Shape=Round Kofun (円墳)| Size=Diameter aqqrox. 33 meters| Construction Period=Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}| Burial Facility=Two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} in [[Shōwa]] 53 (1978)| Special Features=Decorated tumulus }}
'''Maruyamazuka Kofun''' (丸山塚古墳) is a round tumulus ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} and has been designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}.
== Overview == The Maruyamazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus with a diameter of aqqroximately 33 meters and a height of about 5.3 meters. It is believed to have originally been surrounded by a moat and embankment<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>. Located 200 meters north of this tumulus are the {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}, both of which are also designated as national historic sites<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>.
The burial facility is a two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, measuring 8 meters in total length and opening to the south<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>. Similar to the {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}, it features a multi-chamber structure with a front and rear room. Large stones were used for the base, while flat stones were stacked in a dome shape using the split-stone construction technique. Additionally, the inner walls of the back wall, burial chamber, and entrance chamber were decorated with patterns such as triangular motifs, circular designs, and fern patterns in red, yellow, and green. To preserve the stone chamber, it was reburied after investigation, and entry is currently not allowed<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
The Maruyamazuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, slightly later than the {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}} in {{ill|Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|広川町}}, based on the multi-chamber structure of the stone chamber<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Maruyamazuka Kofun| image={{image requested|cat=Yame City}}| Cluster={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}}| location={{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan| Coordinates={{ill|Coordinates|ja|座標}}: {{Coord|33|13|57.6|N|130|34|09.4|E}}| Shape=Round Kofun (円墳)| Size=Diameter aqqrox. 33 meters| Construction Period=Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}| Burial Facility=Two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} in [[Shōwa]] 53 (1978)| Special Features=Decorated tumulus }}
'''Maruyamazuka Kofun''' (丸山塚古墳) is a round tumulus ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} and has been designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}.
== Overview == The Maruyamazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus with a diameter of aqqroximately 33 meters and a height of about 5.3 meters. It is believed to have originally been surrounded by a moat and embankment<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>. Located 200 meters north of this tumulus are the {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}, both of which are also designated as national historic sites<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>.
The burial facility is a two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, measuring 8 meters in total length and opening to the south<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>. Similar to the {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}, it features a multi-chamber structure with a front and rear room. Large stones were used for the base, while flat stones were stacked in a dome shape using the split-stone construction technique. Additionally, the inner walls of the back wall, burial chamber, and entrance chamber were decorated with patterns such as triangular motifs, circular designs, and fern patterns in red, yellow, and green. To preserve the stone chamber, it was reburied after investigation, and entry is currently not allowed<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
The Maruyamazuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, slightly later than the {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}} in {{ill|Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|広川町}}, based on the multi-chamber structure of the stone chamber<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
== References == <references />

It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} and has been designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}.

Located 200 meters north of this tumulus are the {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}, both of which are also designated as national historic sites<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>.

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Maruyamazuka Kofun| image={{image requested|cat=Yame City}}| Cluster={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}}| location={{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan| Coordinates={{ill|Coordinates|ja|座標}}: {{Coord|33|13|57.6|N|130|34|09.4|E}}| Shape=Round Kofun (円墳)| Size=Diameter aqqrox. 33 meters| Construction Period=Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}| Burial Facility=Two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} in [[Shōwa]] 53 (1978)| Special Features=Decorated tumulus }}
'''Maruyamazuka Kofun''' (丸山塚古墳) is a round tumulus ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} and has been designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}.
== Overview == The Maruyamazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus with a diameter of aqqroximately 33 meters and a height of about 5.3 meters. It is believed to have originally been surrounded by a moat and embankment<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>. Located 200 meters north of this tumulus are the {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}, both of which are also designated as national historic sites<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>.
The burial facility is a two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, measuring 8 meters in total length and opening to the south<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>. Similar to the {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}, it features a multi-chamber structure with a front and rear room. Large stones were used for the base, while flat stones were stacked in a dome shape using the split-stone construction technique. Additionally, the inner walls of the back wall, burial chamber, and entrance chamber were decorated with patterns such as triangular motifs, circular designs, and fern patterns in red, yellow, and green. To preserve the stone chamber, it was reburied after investigation, and entry is currently not allowed<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
The Maruyamazuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, slightly later than the {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}} in {{ill|Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|広川町}}, based on the multi-chamber structure of the stone chamber<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Maruyamazuka Kofun| image={{image requested|cat=Yame City}}| Cluster={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}}| location={{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan| Coordinates={{ill|Coordinates|ja|座標}}: {{Coord|33|13|57.6|N|130|34|09.4|E}}| Shape=Round Kofun (円墳)| Size=Diameter a11rox. 33 meters| Construction Period=Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}| Burial Facility=Two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} in [[Shōwa]] 53 (1978)| Special Features=Decorated tumulus }}
'''Maruyamazuka Kofun''' (丸山塚古墳) is a round tumulus ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} and has been designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}.
== Overview == The Maruyamazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus with a diameter of a11roximately 33 meters and a height of about 5.3 meters. It is believed to have originally been surrounded by a moat and embankment<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>. Located 200 meters north of this tumulus are the {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}, both of which are also designated as national historic sites<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>.
The burial facility is a two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, measuring 8 meters in total length and opening to the south<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>. Similar to the {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}, it features a multi-chamber structure with a front and rear room. Large stones were used for the base, while flat stones were stacked in a dome shape using the split-stone construction technique. Additionally, the inner walls of the back wall, burial chamber, and entrance chamber were decorated with patterns such as triangular motifs, circular designs, and fern patterns in red, yellow, and green. To preserve the stone chamber, it was reburied after investigation, and entry is currently not allowed<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
The Maruyamazuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, slightly later than the {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}} in {{ill|Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|広川町}}, based on the multi-chamber structure of the stone chamber<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Maruyamazuka Kofun| image={{image requested|cat=Yame City}}| Cluster={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}}| location={{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan| Coordinates={{ill|Coordinates|ja|座標}}: {{Coord|33|13|57.6|N|130|34|09.4|E}}| Shape=Round Kofun (円墳)| Size=Diameter a11rox. 33 meters| Construction Period=Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}| Burial Facility=Two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} in [[Shōwa]] 53 (1978)| Special Features=Decorated tumulus }}
'''Maruyamazuka Kofun''' (丸山塚古墳) is a round tumulus ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} and has been designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}.
== Overview == The Maruyamazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 33 meters and a height of about 5.3 meters. It is believed to have originally been surrounded by a moat and embankment<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>. Located 200 meters north of this tumulus are the {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}, both of which are also designated as national historic sites<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>.
The burial facility is a two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, measuring 8 meters in total length and opening to the south<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>. Similar to the {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}, it features a multi-chamber structure with a front and rear room. Large stones were used for the base, while flat stones were stacked in a dome shape using the split-stone construction technique. Additionally, the inner walls of the back wall, burial chamber, and entrance chamber were decorated with patterns such as triangular motifs, circular designs, and fern patterns in red, yellow, and green. To preserve the stone chamber, it was reburied after investigation, and entry is currently not allowed<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
The Maruyamazuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, slightly later than the {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}} in {{ill|Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|広川町}}, based on the multi-chamber structure of the stone chamber<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}| name=Maruyamazuka Kofun| image={{image requested|cat=Yame City}}| Cluster={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}}| location={{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan| Coordinates={{ill|Coordinates|ja|座標}}: {{Coord|33|13|57.6|N|130|34|09.4|E}}| Shape=Round Kofun (円墳)| Size=Diameter a11rox. 33 meters| Construction Period=Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}| Burial Facility=Two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} in [[Shōwa]] 53 (1978)| Special Features=Decorated tumulus }}
'''Maruyamazuka Kofun''' (丸山塚古墳) is a round tumulus ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} and has been designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}.
== Overview == The Maruyamazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 33 meters and a height of about 5.3 meters. It is believed to have originally been surrounded by a moat and embankment<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>. Located 200 meters north of this tumulus are the {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}, both of which are also designated as national historic sites<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>.
The burial facility is a two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, measuring 8 meters in total length and opening to the south<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>. Similar to the {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}, it features a multi-chamber structure with a front and rear room. Large stones were used for the base, while flat stones were stacked in a dome shape using the split-stone construction technique. Additionally, the inner walls of the back wall, burial chamber, and entrance chamber were decorated with patterns such as triangular motifs, circular designs, and fern patterns in red, yellow, and green. To preserve the stone chamber, it was reburied after investigation, and entry is currently not allowed<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
The Maruyamazuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, slightly later than the {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}} in {{ill|Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|広川町}}, based on the multi-chamber structure of the stone chamber<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}| name=Maruyamazuka Kofun| image={{image requested|cat=Yame City}}| Cluster={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}}| location={{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan| Coordinates={{ill|Coordinates|ja|座標}}: {{Coord|33|13|57.6|N|130|34|09.4|E}}| Shape=Round Kofun (円墳)| Size=Diameter a11rox. 33 meters| Construction Period=Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}| Burial Facility=Two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} in [[Shōwa]] 53 (1978)| Special Features=Decorated tumulus }}
'''Maruyamazuka Kofun''' (丸山塚古墳) is a round tumulus ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} and has been designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}.
== Overview == The Maruyamazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 33 meters and a height of about 5.3 meters. It is believed to have originally been surrounded by a moat and embankment<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>. Located 200 meters north of this tumulus are the {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}, both of which are also designated as national historic sites<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>.
The burial facility is a two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, measuring 8 meters in total length and opening to the south<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>. Similar to the {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}, it features a multi-chamber structure with a front and rear room. Large stones were used for the base, while flat stones were stacked in a dome shape using the split-stone construction technique. Additionally, the inner walls of the back wall, burial chamber, and entrance chamber were decorated with patterns such as triangular motifs, circular designs, and fern patterns in red, yellow, and green. To preserve the stone chamber, it was reburied after investigation, and entry is currently not allowed<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
The Maruyamazuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, slightly later than the {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}} in {{ill|Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|広川町}}, based on the multi-chamber structure of the stone chamber<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}| name=Maruyamazuka Kofun| image={{image requested|cat=Yame City}}| Cluster={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}}| location={{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan| Coordinates={{ill|Coordinates|ja|座標}}: {{Coord|33|13|57.6|N|130|34|09.4|E}}| Shape=Round Kofun (円墳)| Size=Diameter a11rox. 33 meters| Construction Period=Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}| Burial Facility=Two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} in [[Shōwa]] 53 (1978)| Special Features=Decorated tumulus }}
'''Maruyamazuka Kofun''' (丸山塚古墳) is a round tumulus ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} and has been designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}.
== Overview == The Maruyamazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 33 meters and a height of about 5.3 meters. It is believed to have originally been surrounded by a moat and embankment<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>. Located 200 meters north of this tumulus are the {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}, both of which are also designated as national historic sites<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>.
The burial facility is a two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, measuring 8 meters in total length and opening to the south<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>. Similar to the {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}, it features a multi-chamber structure with a front and rear room. Large stones were used for the base, while flat stones were stacked in a dome shape using the split-stone construction technique. Additionally, the inner walls of the back wall, burial chamber, and entrance chamber were decorated with patterns such as triangular motifs, circular designs, and fern patterns in red, yellow, and green. To preserve the stone chamber, it was reburied after investigation, and entry is currently not allowed<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
The Maruyamazuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, slightly later than the {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}} in {{ill|Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|広川町}}, based on the multi-chamber structure of the stone chamber<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}| name=Maruyamazuka Kofun| image={{image requested|cat=Yame City}}| Cluster={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}}| location={{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan| Coordinates={{ill|Coordinates|ja|座標}}: {{Coord|33|13|57.6|N|130|34|09.4|E}}| Shape=Round Kofun (円墳)| Size=Diameter a11rox. 33 meters| Construction Period=Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}| Burial Facility=Two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} in [[Shōwa]] 53 (1978)| Special Features=Decorated tumulus }}
'''Maruyamazuka Kofun''' (丸山塚古墳) is a round tumulus ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} and has been designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}.
== Overview == The Maruyamazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 33 meters and a height of about 5.3 meters. It is believed to have originally been surrounded by a moat and embankment<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>. Located 200 meters north of this tumulus are the {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}, both of which are also designated as national historic sites<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>.
The burial facility is a two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, measuring 8 meters in total length and opening to the south<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>. Similar to the {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}, it features a multi-chamber structure with a front and rear room. Large stones were used for the base, while flat stones were stacked in a dome shape using the split-stone construction technique. Additionally, the inner walls of the back wall, burial chamber, and entrance chamber were decorated with patterns such as triangular motifs, circular designs, and fern patterns in red, yellow, and green. To preserve the stone chamber, it was reburied after investigation, and entry is currently not allowed<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
The Maruyamazuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, slightly later than the {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}} in {{ill|Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|広川町}}, based on the multi-chamber structure of the stone chamber<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|円墳" to "|円墳|1=Round Kofun"
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}| name=Maruyamazuka Kofun| image={{image requested|cat=Yame City}}| Cluster={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}}| location={{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan| Coordinates={{ill|Coordinates|ja|座標}}: {{Coord|33|13|57.6|N|130|34|09.4|E}}| Shape=Round Kofun (円墳)| Size=Diameter a11rox. 33 meters| Construction Period=Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}| Burial Facility=Two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} in [[Shōwa]] 53 (1978)| Special Features=Decorated tumulus }}
'''Maruyamazuka Kofun''' (丸山塚古墳) is a round tumulus ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} and has been designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}.
== Overview == The Maruyamazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 33 meters and a height of about 5.3 meters. It is believed to have originally been surrounded by a moat and embankment<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>. Located 200 meters north of this tumulus are the {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}, both of which are also designated as national historic sites<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>.
The burial facility is a two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, measuring 8 meters in total length and opening to the south<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>. Similar to the {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}, it features a multi-chamber structure with a front and rear room. Large stones were used for the base, while flat stones were stacked in a dome shape using the split-stone construction technique. Additionally, the inner walls of the back wall, burial chamber, and entrance chamber were decorated with patterns such as triangular motifs, circular designs, and fern patterns in red, yellow, and green. To preserve the stone chamber, it was reburied after investigation, and entry is currently not allowed<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
The Maruyamazuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, slightly later than the {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}} in {{ill|Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|広川町}}, based on the multi-chamber structure of the stone chamber<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
== References == <references />
== See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan#Fukuoka Prefecture]] * [[List of historic sites in Kyushu and Okinawa#Fukuoka Prefecture]] * [[:en:Decorated_kofun|Decorated tumulus]]

{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}| name=Maruyamazuka Kofun| image={{image requested|cat=Yame City}}| Cluster={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}}| location={{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan| Coordinates={{ill|Coordinates|ja|座標}}: {{Coord|33|13|57.6|N|130|34|09.4|E}}| Shape=Round Kofun (円墳)| Size=Diameter a11rox. 33 meters| Construction Period=Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}| Burial Facility=Two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} in [[Shōwa]] 53 (1978)| Special Features=Decorated tumulus }}
'''Maruyamazuka Kofun''' (丸山塚古墳) is a round tumulus ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} and has been designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}.
== Overview == The Maruyamazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 33 meters and a height of about 5.3 meters. It is believed to have originally been surrounded by a moat and embankment<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>. Located 200 meters north of this tumulus are the {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}, both of which are also designated as national historic sites<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>.
The burial facility is a two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, measuring 8 meters in total length and opening to the south<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>. Similar to the {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}, it features a multi-chamber structure with a front and rear room. Large stones were used for the base, while flat stones were stacked in a dome shape using the split-stone construction technique. Additionally, the inner walls of the back wall, burial chamber, and entrance chamber were decorated with patterns such as triangular motifs, circular designs, and fern patterns in red, yellow, and green. To preserve the stone chamber, it was reburied after investigation, and entry is currently not allowed<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
The Maruyamazuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, slightly later than the {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}} in {{ill|Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|広川町}}, based on the multi-chamber structure of the stone chamber<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.

{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}| name=Maruyamazuka Kofun| image={{image requested|cat=Yame City}}| Cluster={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}}| location={{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan| Coordinates={{ill|Coordinates|ja|座標}}: {{Coord|33|13|57.6|N|130|34|09.4|E}}| Shape=Round Kofun (円墳)| Size=Diameter a11rox. 33 meters| Construction Period=Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}| Burial Facility=Two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} in [[Shōwa]] 53 (1978)| Special Features=Decorated tumulus }}
'''Maruyamazuka Kofun''' (丸山塚古墳) is a round tumulus ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} and has been designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}.
== Overview == The Maruyamazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 33 meters and a height of about 5.3 meters. It is believed to have originally been surrounded by a moat and embankment<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>. Located 200 meters north of this tumulus are the {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}, both of which are also designated as national historic sites<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>.
The burial facility is a two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, measuring 8 meters in total length and opening to the south<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>. Similar to the {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}, it features a multi-chamber structure with a front and rear room. Large stones were used for the base, while flat stones were stacked in a dome shape using the split-stone construction technique. Additionally, the inner walls of the back wall, burial chamber, and entrance chamber were decorated with patterns such as triangular motifs, circular designs, and fern patterns in red, yellow, and green. To preserve the stone chamber, it was reburied after investigation, and entry is currently not allowed<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
The Maruyamazuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, slightly later than the {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}} in {{ill|Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|広川町}}, based on the multi-chamber structure of the stone chamber<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
== References == <references />

{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}| name=Maruyamazuka Kofun| image={{image requested|cat=Yame City}}| Cluster={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}}| location={{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan| Coordinates={{ill|Coordinates|ja|座標}}: {{Coord|33|13|57.6|N|130|34|09.4|E}}| Shape=Round Kofun (円墳)| Size=Diameter a11rox. 33 meters| Construction Period=Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}| Burial Facility=Two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} in [[Shōwa]] 53 (1978)| Special Features=Decorated tumulus }}
'''Maruyamazuka Kofun''' (丸山塚古墳) is a round tumulus ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} and has been designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}.
== Overview == The Maruyamazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 33 meters and a height of about 5.3 meters. It is believed to have originally been surrounded by a moat and embankment<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>. Located 200 meters north of this tumulus are the {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}, both of which are also designated as national historic sites<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>.
The burial facility is a two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, measuring 8 meters in total length and opening to the south<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>. Similar to the {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}, it features a multi-chamber structure with a front and rear room. Large stones were used for the base, while flat stones were stacked in a dome shape using the split-stone construction technique. Additionally, the inner walls of the back wall, burial chamber, and entrance chamber were decorated with patterns such as triangular motifs, circular designs, and fern patterns in red, yellow, and green. To preserve the stone chamber, it was reburied after investigation, and entry is currently not allowed<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
The Maruyamazuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, slightly later than the {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}} in {{ill|Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|広川町}}, based on the multi-chamber structure of the stone chamber<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.

It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} and has been designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}.
== Overview == The Maruyamazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 33 meters and a height of about 5.3 meters. It is believed to have originally been surrounded by a moat and embankment<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>. Located 200 meters north of this tumulus are the {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}, both of which are also designated as national historic sites<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>.
The burial facility is a two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, measuring 8 meters in total length and opening to the south<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>. Similar to the {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}, it features a multi-chamber structure with a front and rear room. Large stones were used for the base, while flat stones were stacked in a dome shape using the split-stone construction technique. Additionally, the inner walls of the back wall, burial chamber, and entrance chamber were decorated with patterns such as triangular motifs, circular designs, and fern patterns in red, yellow, and green. To preserve the stone chamber, it was reburied after investigation, and entry is currently not allowed<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
The Maruyamazuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, slightly later than the {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}} in {{ill|Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|広川町}}, based on the multi-chamber structure of the stone chamber<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.

'''Maruyamazuka Kofun''' (丸山塚古墳) is a round tumulus ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} and has been designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}.
== Overview == The Maruyamazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 33 meters and a height of about 5.3 meters. It is believed to have originally been surrounded by a moat and embankment<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>. Located 200 meters north of this tumulus are the {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}, both of which are also designated as national historic sites<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>.
The burial facility is a two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, measuring 8 meters in total length and opening to the south<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>. Similar to the {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}, it features a multi-chamber structure with a front and rear room. Large stones were used for the base, while flat stones were stacked in a dome shape using the split-stone construction technique. Additionally, the inner walls of the back wall, burial chamber, and entrance chamber were decorated with patterns such as triangular motifs, circular designs, and fern patterns in red, yellow, and green. To preserve the stone chamber, it was reburied after investigation, and entry is currently not allowed<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
The Maruyamazuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, slightly later than the {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}} in {{ill|Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|広川町}}, based on the multi-chamber structure of the stone chamber<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.

'''Maruyamazuka Kofun''' (丸山塚古墳) is a round tumulus ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} and has been designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}.

Located 200 meters north of this tumulus are the {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}, both of which are also designated as national historic sites<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>.

Similar to the {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}, it features a multi-chamber structure with a front and rear room. Large stones were used for the base, while flat stones were stacked in a dome shape using the split-stone construction technique. Additionally, the inner walls of the back wall, burial chamber, and entrance chamber were decorated with patterns such as triangular motifs, circular designs, and fern patterns in red, yellow, and green. To preserve the stone chamber, it was reburied after investigation, and entry is currently not allowed<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
The Maruyamazuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, slightly later than the {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}} in {{ill|Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|広川町}}, based on the multi-chamber structure of the stone chamber<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also! ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}| name=Maruyamazuka Kofun| image={{image requested|cat=Yame City}}| Cluster={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}}| location={{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan| Coordinates={{ill|Coordinates|ja|座標}}: {{Coord|33|13|57.6|N|130|34|09.4|E}}| Shape=Round Kofun (円墳)| Size=Diameter a11rox. 33 meters| Construction Period=Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}| Burial Facility=Two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} in [[Shōwa]] 53 (1978)| Special Features=Decorated tumulus }}
'''Maruyamazuka Kofun''' (丸山塚古墳) is a round tumulus ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群}} and has been designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}.
== Overview == The Maruyamazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 33 meters and a height of about 5.3 meters. It is believed to have originally been surrounded by a moat and embankment<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>. Located 200 meters north of this tumulus are the {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}, both of which are also designated as national historic sites<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>.
The burial facility is a two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, measuring 8 meters in total length and opening to the south<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>. Similar to the {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳}}, it features a multi-chamber structure with a front and rear room. Large stones were used for the base, while flat stones were stacked in a dome shape using the split-stone construction technique. Additionally, the inner walls of the back wall, burial chamber, and entrance chamber were decorated with patterns such as triangular motifs, circular designs, and fern patterns in red, yellow, and green. To preserve the stone chamber, it was reburied after investigation, and entry is currently not allowed<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
The Maruyamazuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, slightly later than the {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}} in {{ill|Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|広川町}}, based on the multi-chamber structure of the stone chamber<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
== References == <references /> == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan#Fukuoka Prefecture]] * [[List of historic sites in Kyushu and Okinawa#Fukuoka Prefecture]] * [[:en:Decorated_kofun|Decorated tumulus]]
== External Links == * [http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/maruyamaduka Maruyamazuka Kofun - Iwato Historical Museum] * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/aboutyame/bunka/k_i_006.html Maruyamazuka Kofun - Yame City Official Website] * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=29&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Maruyamazuka Kofun] - Decorated Kofun Database ({{ill|Kyushu National Museum|ja|九州国立博物館}})
