{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}}| name=Maruyamazuka Kofun| image={{image requested|cat=Yame City}}| Cluster={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群|12=draft}}| location={{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|12=draft}}, Japan| coordinates={{ill|coordinates|ja|座標|12=draft}}: {{Coord|33|13|57.6|N|130|34|09.4|E}}| type=Round Kofun (円墳)| area=Diameter a11rox. 33 meters| built=Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}}| burial=Two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}| Historic site =Designated as a national {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} in [[Shōwa]] 53 (1978)| Special Features=Decorated tumulus }}
'''Maruyamazuka Kofun''' (丸山塚古墳) is a round tumulus ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}}) located in {{ill|Yame City|ja|八女市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|12=draft}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|ja|八女古墳群|12=draft}} and has been Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}}.
== Overview == The Maruyamazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus with a diameter of approximately 33 meters and a height of about 5.3 meters. It is believed to have originally been surrounded by a moat and embankment<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>. Located 200 meters north of this tumulus are the {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|12=draft}}, both of which are also Historic site as national historic sites<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>.
The burial facility is a two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, measuring 8 meters in total length and opening to the south<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>. Similar to the {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|12=draft}}, it features a multi-chamber structure with a front and rear room. Large stones were used for the base, while flat stones were stacked in a dome type using the split-stone construction technique. Additionally, the inner walls of the back wall, burial chamber, and entrance chamber were decorated with patterns such as triangular motifs, circular designs, and fern patterns in red, yellow, and green. To preserve the stone chamber, it was reburied after investigation, and entry is currently not allowed<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
The Maruyamazuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}}, slightly later than the {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|広川町|12=draft}}, based on the multi-chamber structure of the stone chamber<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
== References == <references />

9 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:丸山塚古墳 (八女市) up to rev 91173018
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳| 名称=丸山塚古墳| 画像 ={{画像募集中|cat=八女市}}| 所属=[[八女古墳群]]| 所在地=福岡県八女市宅間田| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|13|57.6|N|130|34|09.4|E|}}| 形状=円墳| 規模=直径約33m| 築造年代=6世紀後半| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 史跡指定=[[昭和]]53年（[[1978年]]）国の[[史跡]]| 特記事項=装飾古墳}}
'''丸山塚古墳'''（まるやまづかこふん）は、[[福岡県]][[八女市]]宅間田に所在する[[円墳]]である。国の[[史跡]]に指定。[[八女古墳群]]を構成する1基。
== 概要 == 直径約33m・高さ約5.3mを測る大型の円墳で、本来は墳丘を取り巻く周濠・周堤が存在したと思われる<ref name=iwato>「岩戸山歴史資料館 展示図録」27頁</ref>。北側200mの所には、[[善蔵塚古墳]]と[[茶臼塚古墳]]（共に国の史跡）がある<ref name=genchi>現地説明版</ref>。
埋葬施設は両袖型横穴式石室で、全長8mを測り南に開口する<ref name=genchi>現地説明版</ref>。[[乗場古墳]]と同様、前室と後室に分かれた複室構造で、腰石に大きな石材を用い、上部には扁平の石材を用い割石積みをし、ドーム状に積み上げている。また、奥壁や玄室・前室の袖石には赤・黄・緑の三色により三角文・円文・蕨手文が描かれている。石室は保存のため調査後に埋め戻され、現在入ることは出来ない<ref name=iwato>「岩戸山歴史資料館 展示図録」27頁</ref>。
丸山塚古墳が造営された時期は、石室が複室構造であることなどから[[広川町 (福岡県)|広川町]]の[[弘化谷古墳]]よりやや後出するものと考えられ、6世紀後半頃と推定されている<ref name=iwato>「岩戸山歴史資料館 展示図録」27頁</ref>。
== 脚注 == <references />
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧#福岡県]] * [[九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県]] * [[装飾古墳]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/maruyamaduka 丸山塚古墳 岩戸山歴史資料館] * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/aboutyame/bunka/k_i_006.html 丸山塚古墳 八女市] * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=29&modori=mjl&ACD=7 丸山塚古墳] - 装飾古墳データベース（[[九州国立博物館]]）

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳| 名称=丸山塚古墳| 画像 ={{画像募集中|cat=八女市}}| 所属=[[八女古墳群]]| 所在地=福岡県八女市宅間田| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|13|57.6|N|130|34|09.4|E|}}| 形状=円墳| 規模=直径約33m| 築造年代=6世紀後半| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 史跡指定=[[昭和]]53年（[[1978年]]）国の[[史跡]]| 特記事項=装飾古墳}}
'''丸山塚古墳'''（まるやまづかこふん）は、[[福岡県]][[八女市]]宅間田に所在する[[円墳]]である。国の[[史跡]]に指定。[[八女古墳群]]を構成する1基。
== 概要 == 直径約33m・高さ約5.3mを測る大型の円墳で、本来は墳丘を取り巻く周濠・周堤が存在したと思われる<ref name=iwato>「岩戸山歴史資料館 展示図録」27頁</ref>。北側200mの所には、[[善蔵塚古墳]]と[[茶臼塚古墳]]（共に国の史跡）がある<ref name=genchi>現地説明版</ref>。
埋葬施設は両袖型横穴式石室で、全長8mを測り南に開口する<ref name=genchi>現地説明版</ref>。[[乗場古墳]]と同様、前室と後室に分かれた複室構造で、腰石に大きな石材を用い、上部には扁平の石材を用い割石積みをし、ドーム状に積み上げている。また、奥壁や玄室・前室の袖石には赤・黄・緑の三色により三角文・円文・蕨手文が描かれている。石室は保存のため調査後に埋め戻され、現在入ることは出来ない<ref name=iwato>「岩戸山歴史資料館 展示図録」27頁</ref>。
丸山塚古墳が造営された時期は、石室が複室構造であることなどから[[広川町 (福岡県)|広川町]]の[[弘化谷古墳]]よりやや後出するものと考えられ、6世紀後半頃と推定されている<ref name=iwato>「岩戸山歴史資料館 展示図録」27頁</ref>。
== 脚注 == <references />
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧#福岡県]] * [[九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県]] * [[装飾古墳]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/maruyamaduka 丸山塚古墳 岩戸山歴史資料館] * [http://www.city.yame.fukuoka.jp/aboutyame/bunka/k_i_006.html 丸山塚古墳 八女市] * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=29&modori=mjl&ACD=7 丸山塚古墳] - 装飾古墳データベース（[[九州国立博物館]]）

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}| name=Maruyamazuka Kofun| image={{image requested|cat=Yame City}}| Cluster=[[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Cluster]]| location={{ill|Yame|qid=Q850492}}| coordinates={{Coord|33.232666666667|N|130.56927777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| type=Round Kofun (円墳)| area=Diameter a11rox. 33 meters| built=Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]]| burial=Two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}| Historic site =Designated as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} in [[Shōwa]] 53 (1978)| Special Features=Decorated tumulus | architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|qid=Q11394747}}| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|qid=Q64615207}}}}
'''Maruyamazuka Kofun''' (丸山塚古墳) is a round tumulus ([[:en:Kofun|Kofun]]) located in [[:en:Yame|Yame City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Cluster]] and has been Historic site as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}.
== Overview == The Maruyamazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus with a diameter of approximately 33 meters and a height of about 5.3 meters. It is believed to have originally been surrounded by a moat and embankment<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>. Located 200 meters north of this tumulus are the {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuzuka Kofun|qq=}}, both of which are also Historic site as national historic sites<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>.
The burial facility is a two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, measuring 8 meters in total length and opening to the south<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>. Similar to the {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}}, it features a multi-chamber structure with a front and rear room. Large stones were used for the base, while flat stones were stacked in a dome type using the split-stone construction technique. Additionally, the inner walls of the back wall, burial chamber, and entrance chamber were decorated with patterns such as triangular motifs, circular designs, and fern patterns in red, yellow, and green. To preserve the stone chamber, it was reburied after investigation, and entry is currently not allowed<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
The Maruyamazuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], slightly later than the {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkadani Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|広川町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|qq=|1=hirogawachou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485375}}, based on the multi-chamber structure of the stone chamber<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
== References == <references />

'''Maruyamazuka Kofun''' (丸山塚古墳) is a round tumulus ([[:en:Kofun|Kofun]]) located in [[:en:Yame|Yame City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Cluster]] and has been Historic site as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}.

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}| name=Maruyamazuka Kofun| image={{image requested|cat=Yame City}}| Cluster=[[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Cluster]]| location={{ill|Yame|WD=Q850492}}| coordinates={{Coord|33.232666666667|N|130.56927777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| type=Round Kofun (円墳)| area=Diameter a11rox. 33 meters| built=Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]]| burial=Two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}| Historic site =Designated as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} in [[Shōwa]] 53 (1978)| Special Features=Decorated tumulus | architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}}| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|WD=Q64615207}}}}
'''Maruyamazuka Kofun''' (丸山塚古墳) is a round tumulus ([[:en:Kofun|Kofun]]) located in [[:en:Yame|Yame City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Cluster]] and has been Historic site as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}.
== Overview == The Maruyamazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus with a diameter of approximately 33 meters and a height of about 5.3 meters. It is believed to have originally been surrounded by a moat and embankment<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>. Located 200 meters north of this tumulus are the {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuzuka Kofun|qq=}}, both of which are also Historic site as national historic sites<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>.
The burial facility is a two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, measuring 8 meters in total length and opening to the south<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>. Similar to the {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}}, it features a multi-chamber structure with a front and rear room. Large stones were used for the base, while flat stones were stacked in a dome type using the split-stone construction technique. Additionally, the inner walls of the back wall, burial chamber, and entrance chamber were decorated with patterns such as triangular motifs, circular designs, and fern patterns in red, yellow, and green. To preserve the stone chamber, it was reburied after investigation, and entry is currently not allowed<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
The Maruyamazuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], slightly later than the {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkadani Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|広川町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|qq=|1=hirogawachou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485375}}, based on the multi-chamber structure of the stone chamber<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}| name=Maruyamazuka Kofun| image={{image requested|cat=Yame City}}| Cluster=[[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Cluster]]| location={{ill|Yame|WD=Q850492}}| coordinates={{Coord|33.232666666667|N|130.56927777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| type=Round Kofun (円墳)| area=Diameter a11rox. 33 meters| built=Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]]| burial=Two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}| Historic site =Designated as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} in [[Shōwa]] 53 (1978)| Special Features=Decorated tumulus | architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}}| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|WD=Q64615207}}}}
'''Maruyamazuka Kofun''' (丸山塚古墳) is a round tumulus ([[:en:Kofun|Kofun]]) located in [[:en:Yame|Yame City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Cluster]] and has been Historic site as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}.
== Overview == The Maruyamazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus with a diameter of approximately 33 meters and a height of about 5.3 meters. It is believed to have originally been surrounded by a moat and embankment<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>. Located 200 meters north of this tumulus are the {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuzuka Kofun|qq=}}, both of which are also Historic site as national historic sites<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>.
The burial facility is a two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, measuring 8 meters in total length and opening to the south<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>. Similar to the {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}}, it features a multi-chamber structure with a front and rear room. Large stones were used for the base, while flat stones were stacked in a dome type using the split-stone construction technique. Additionally, the inner walls of the back wall, burial chamber, and entrance chamber were decorated with patterns such as triangular motifs, circular designs, and fern patterns in red, yellow, and green. To preserve the stone chamber, it was reburied after investigation, and entry is currently not allowed<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
The Maruyamazuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], slightly later than the {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkadani Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|広川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|1=hirogawachou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485375}}, based on the multi-chamber structure of the stone chamber<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}| name=Maruyamazuka Kofun| image={{image requested|cat=Yame City}}| Cluster=[[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Cluster]]| location={{ill|Yame|WD=Q850492}}| coordinates={{Coord|33.232666666667|N|130.56927777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| type=Round Kofun (円墳)| area=Diameter a11rox. 33 meters| built=Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]]| burial=Two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}| Historic site =Designated as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} in [[Shōwa]] 53 (1978)| Special Features=Decorated tumulus | architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}}| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|WD=Q64615207}}}}
'''Maruyamazuka Kofun''' (丸山塚古墳) is a round tumulus ([[:en:Kofun|Kofun]]) located in [[:en:Yame|Yame City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Cluster]] and has been Historic site as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}.
== Overview == The Maruyamazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus with a diameter of approximately 33 meters and a height of about 5.3 meters. It is believed to have originally been surrounded by a moat and embankment<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>. Located 200 meters north of this tumulus are the {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuzuka Kofun|qq=}}, both of which are also Historic site as national historic sites<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>.
The burial facility is a two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, measuring 8 meters in total length and opening to the south<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>. Similar to the {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}}, it features a multi-chamber structure with a front and rear room. Large stones were used for the base, while flat stones were stacked in a dome type using the split-stone construction technique. Additionally, the inner walls of the back wall, burial chamber, and entrance chamber were decorated with patterns such as triangular motifs, circular designs, and fern patterns in red, yellow, and green. To preserve the stone chamber, it was reburied after investigation, and entry is currently not allowed<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
The Maruyamazuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], slightly later than the {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkadani Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|広川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|1=hirogawachou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485375}}, based on the multi-chamber structure of the stone chamber<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}| name=Maruyamazuka Kofun| image={{image requested|cat=Yame City}}| Cluster=[[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Cluster]]| location={{ill|Yame|WD=Q850492}}| coordinates={{Coord|33.232666666667|N|130.56927777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| type=Round Kofun (円墳)| area=Diameter a11rox. 33 meters| built=Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]]| burial=Two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}| Historic site =Designated as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} in [[Shōwa]] 53 (1978)| Special Features=Decorated tumulus | architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}}| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|WD=Q64615207}}}}
'''Maruyamazuka Kofun''' (丸山塚古墳) is a round tumulus ([[:en:Kofun|Kofun]]) located in [[:en:Yame|Yame City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Cluster]] and has been Historic site as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}.
== Overview == The Maruyamazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus with a diameter of approximately 33 meters and a height of about 5.3 meters. It is believed to have originally been surrounded by a moat and embankment<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>. Located 200 meters north of this tumulus are the {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuzuka Kofun|qq=}}, both of which are also Historic site as national historic sites<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>.
The burial facility is a two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, measuring 8 meters in total length and opening to the south<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>. Similar to the {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}}, it features a multi-chamber structure with a front and rear room. Large stones were used for the base, while flat stones were stacked in a dome type using the split-stone construction technique. Additionally, the inner walls of the back wall, burial chamber, and entrance chamber were decorated with patterns such as triangular motifs, circular designs, and fern patterns in red, yellow, and green. To preserve the stone chamber, it was reburied after investigation, and entry is currently not allowed<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
The Maruyamazuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], slightly later than the {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkadani Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|広川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|1=hirogawachou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485375}}, based on the multi-chamber structure of the stone chamber<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}| name=Maruyamazuka Kofun| image={{image requested|cat=Yame City}}| Cluster=[[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Cluster]]| location={{ill|Yame|WD=Q850492}}| coordinates={{Coord|33.232666666667|N|130.56927777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| type=Round Kofun (円墳)| area=Diameter a11rox. 33 meters| built=Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]]| burial=Two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}| Historic site =Designated as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} in [[Shōwa]] 53 (1978)| Special Features=Decorated tumulus | architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}}| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|WD=Q64615207}}}}
'''Maruyamazuka Kofun''' (丸山塚古墳) is a round tumulus ([[:en:Kofun|Kofun]]) located in [[:en:Yame|Yame City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Cluster]] and has been Historic site as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}.
== Overview == The Maruyamazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus with a diameter of approximately 33 meters and a height of about 5.3 meters. It is believed to have originally been surrounded by a moat and embankment<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>. Located 200 meters north of this tumulus are the {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuzuka Kofun|qq=}}, both of which are also Historic site as national historic sites<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>.
The burial facility is a two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, measuring 8 meters in total length and opening to the south<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>. Similar to the {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}}, it features a multi-chamber structure with a front and rear room. Large stones were used for the base, while flat stones were stacked in a dome type using the split-stone construction technique. Additionally, the inner walls of the back wall, burial chamber, and entrance chamber were decorated with patterns such as triangular motifs, circular designs, and fern patterns in red, yellow, and green. To preserve the stone chamber, it was reburied after investigation, and entry is currently not allowed<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
The Maruyamazuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], slightly later than the {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkadani Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|広川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|1=hirogawachou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485375}}, based on the multi-chamber structure of the stone chamber<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.

Additionally, the inner walls of the back wall, burial chamber, and entrance chamber were decorated with patterns such as triangular motifs, circular designs, and fern patterns in red, yellow, and green. To preserve the stone chamber, it was reburied after investigation, and entry is currently not allowed<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
The Maruyamazuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], slightly later than the {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkadani Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|広川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|1=hirogawachou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485375}}, based on the multi-chamber structure of the stone chamber<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}| name=Maruyamazuka Kofun| image={{image requested|cat=Yame City}}| Cluster=[[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Cluster]]| location={{ill|Yame|WD=Q850492}}| coordinates={{Coord|33.232666666667|N|130.56927777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| type=Round Kofun (円墳)| area=Diameter a11rox. 33 meters| built=Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]]| burial=Two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}| Historic site =Designated as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} in [[Shōwa]] 53 (1978)| Special Features=Decorated tumulus | architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}}| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|WD=Q64615207}}}}
'''Maruyamazuka Kofun''' (丸山塚古墳) is a round tumulus ([[:en:Kofun|Kofun]]) located in [[:en:Yame|Yame City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Cluster]] and has been Historic site as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}.
== Overview == The Maruyamazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus with a diameter of approximately 33 meters and a height of about 5.3 meters. It is believed to have originally been surrounded by a moat and embankment<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>. Located 200 meters north of this tumulus are the {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuzuka Kofun|qq=}}, both of which are also Historic site as national historic sites<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>.
The burial facility is a two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, measuring 8 meters in total length and opening to the south<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>. Similar to the {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}}, it features a multi-chamber structure with a front and rear room. Large stones were used for the base, while flat stones were stacked in a dome type using the split-stone construction technique. Additionally, the inner walls of the back wall, burial chamber, and entrance chamber were decorated with patterns such as triangular motifs, circular designs, and fern patterns in red, yellow, and green. To preserve the stone chamber, it was reburied after investigation, and entry is currently not allowed<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
The Maruyamazuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], slightly later than the {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkadani Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|広川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|1=hirogawachou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485375}}, based on the multi-chamber structure of the stone chamber<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.

It is believed to have originally been surrounded by a moat and embankment<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>. Located 200 meters north of this tumulus are the {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuzuka Kofun|qq=}}, both of which are also Historic site as national historic sites<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>.
The burial facility is a two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, measuring 8 meters in total length and opening to the south<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>. Similar to the {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}}, it features a multi-chamber structure with a front and rear room. Large stones were used for the base, while flat stones were stacked in a dome type using the split-stone construction technique. Additionally, the inner walls of the back wall, burial chamber, and entrance chamber were decorated with patterns such as triangular motifs, circular designs, and fern patterns in red, yellow, and green. To preserve the stone chamber, it was reburied after investigation, and entry is currently not allowed<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
The Maruyamazuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], slightly later than the {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkadani Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|広川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|1=hirogawachou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485375}}, based on the multi-chamber structure of the stone chamber<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}| name=Maruyamazuka Kofun| image={{image requested|cat=Yame City}}| Cluster={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|en|Yame Kofun Cluster|WD=Q64615207|lt=Yame Kofun Cluster}}| location={{ill|Yame|WD=Q850492}}| coordinates={{Coord|33.232666666667|N|130.56927777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| type=Round Kofun (円墳)| area=Diameter a11rox. 33 meters| built=Late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}}| burial=Two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}| Historic site =Designated as a national {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} in [[Shōwa]] 53 (1978)| Special Features=Decorated tumulus | architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}}| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|WD=Q64615207}}}}
'''Maruyamazuka Kofun''' (丸山塚古墳) is a round tumulus ({{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Yame|en|Yame|WD=Q850492|lt=Yame City}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|en|Yame Kofun Cluster|WD=Q64615207|lt=Yame Kofun Cluster}} and has been Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}.
== Overview == The Maruyamazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus with a diameter of approximately 33 meters and a height of about 5.3 meters. It is believed to have originally been surrounded by a moat and embankment<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>. Located 200 meters north of this tumulus are the {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuzuka Kofun|qq=}}, both of which are also Historic site as national historic sites<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>.
The burial facility is a two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, measuring 8 meters in total length and opening to the south<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>. Similar to the {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}}, it features a multi-chamber structure with a front and rear room. Large stones were used for the base, while flat stones were stacked in a dome type using the split-stone construction technique. Additionally, the inner walls of the back wall, burial chamber, and entrance chamber were decorated with patterns such as triangular motifs, circular designs, and fern patterns in red, yellow, and green. To preserve the stone chamber, it was reburied after investigation, and entry is currently not allowed<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
The Maruyamazuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}}, slightly later than the {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkadani Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|広川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|1=hirogawachou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485375}}, based on the multi-chamber structure of the stone chamber<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.

'''Maruyamazuka Kofun''' (丸山塚古墳) is a round tumulus ({{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Yame|en|Yame|WD=Q850492|lt=Yame City}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|en|Yame Kofun Cluster|WD=Q64615207|lt=Yame Kofun Cluster}} and has been Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}.
== Overview == The Maruyamazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus with a diameter of approximately 33 meters and a height of about 5.3 meters. It is believed to have originally been surrounded by a moat and embankment<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>. Located 200 meters north of this tumulus are the {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuzuka Kofun|qq=}}, both of which are also Historic site as national historic sites<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>.
The burial facility is a two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, measuring 8 meters in total length and opening to the south<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>. Similar to the {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}}, it features a multi-chamber structure with a front and rear room. Large stones were used for the base, while flat stones were stacked in a dome type using the split-stone construction technique. Additionally, the inner walls of the back wall, burial chamber, and entrance chamber were decorated with patterns such as triangular motifs, circular designs, and fern patterns in red, yellow, and green. To preserve the stone chamber, it was reburied after investigation, and entry is currently not allowed<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
The Maruyamazuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}}, slightly later than the {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkadani Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|広川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|1=hirogawachou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485375}}, based on the multi-chamber structure of the stone chamber<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.

'''Maruyamazuka Kofun''' (丸山塚古墳) is a round tumulus ({{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Yame|en|Yame|WD=Q850492|lt=Yame City}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|en|Yame Kofun Cluster|WD=Q64615207|lt=Yame Kofun Cluster}} and has been Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}.
== Overview == The Maruyamazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus with a diameter of approximately 33 meters and a height of about 5.3 meters. It is believed to have originally been surrounded by a moat and embankment<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>. Located 200 meters north of this tumulus are the {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuzuka Kofun|qq=}}, both of which are also Historic site as national historic sites<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>.
The burial facility is a two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, measuring 8 meters in total length and opening to the south<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>. Similar to the {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}}, it features a multi-chamber structure with a front and rear room. Large stones were used for the base, while flat stones were stacked in a dome type using the split-stone construction technique. Additionally, the inner walls of the back wall, burial chamber, and entrance chamber were decorated with patterns such as triangular motifs, circular designs, and fern patterns in red, yellow, and green. To preserve the stone chamber, it was reburied after investigation, and entry is currently not allowed<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
The Maruyamazuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}}, slightly later than the {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkadani Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|広川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|1=hirogawachou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485375}}, based on the multi-chamber structure of the stone chamber<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.

'''Maruyamazuka Kofun''' (丸山塚古墳) is a round tumulus ({{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Yame|en|Yame|WD=Q850492|lt=Yame City|1=Yame}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|en|Yame Kofun Cluster|WD=Q64615207|lt=Yame Kofun Cluster|1=Yame Kofun Cluster}} and has been Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}}.
== Overview == The Maruyamazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus with a diameter of approximately 33 meters and a height of about 5.3 meters. It is believed to have originally been surrounded by a moat and embankment<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>. Located 200 meters north of this tumulus are the {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuzuka Kofun|qq=}}, both of which are also Historic site as national historic sites<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>.
The burial facility is a two-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style Stone Chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, measuring 8 meters in total length and opening to the south<ref name=genchi>On-site explanation panel.</ref>. Similar to the {{ill|Noriba Kofun|ja|乗場古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noriba Kofun|qq=}}, it features a multi-chamber structure with a front and rear room. Large stones were used for the base, while flat stones were stacked in a dome type using the split-stone construction technique. Additionally, the inner walls of the back wall, burial chamber, and entrance chamber were decorated with patterns such as triangular motifs, circular designs, and fern patterns in red, yellow, and green. To preserve the stone chamber, it was reburied after investigation, and entry is currently not allowed<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
The Maruyamazuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}}, slightly later than the {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkadani Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|ja|広川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Town (Fukuoka Prefecture)|1=hirogawachou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485375}}, based on the multi-chamber structure of the stone chamber<ref name=iwato>"Iwato Historical Museum Exhibit Catalogue," p. 27.</ref>.
== References == <references />
